name: Rivertown Community Dental Clinic
description: Free and low-cost dental care for children and adults. Sliding scale fees based on income. Offers cleanings extractions and emergency dental pain relief.
eligibility: No insurance required; sliding scale; children and adults welcome

name: Northside Family Health Center
description: Full primary care clinic offering pediatric checkups vaccinations and chronic disease management. Bilingual staff on site.
eligibility: Uninsured and underinsured accepted; families prioritized

name: Rivertown Free Pharmacy Program
description: Provides free prescription medications for qualifying low-income residents including diabetes and blood pressure medication.
eligibility: Household income under 200% federal poverty line; ID required

name: Hillcrest Mental Health Counseling
description: Individual and group therapy sessions on a sliding scale. Specializes in anxiety depression and trauma counseling for teens and adults.
eligibility: Sliding scale fee; no insurance required; ages 13+

name: Rivertown Crisis Counseling Hotline
description: 24/7 phone counseling for mental health crises including suicide prevention and emotional support. Free and confidential.
eligibility: Free; open to anyone; no eligibility requirements

name: Downtown Youth Counseling Center
description: Counseling services specifically for children and teenagers dealing with anxiety school stress and family issues.
eligibility: Ages 5-18; parental consent required; sliding scale

name: Rivertown Emergency Shelter
description: Overnight emergency shelter beds for individuals and families experiencing homelessness. Meals provided.
eligibility: Open to all; families with children given priority; walk-ins accepted after 4pm

name: Safe Haven Domestic Violence Shelter
description: Confidential emergency shelter for individuals and families fleeing domestic violence. Includes safety planning and legal advocacy.
eligibility: Survivors of domestic violence and their children; confidential intake

name: Rivertown Winter Warming Center
description: Seasonal overnight shelter open during cold weather months providing beds blankets and hot meals.
eligibility: Open to all; seasonal Nov-Mar only

name: St. Mary's Food Pantry
description: Weekly grocery distribution including fresh produce canned goods and baby formula. No proof of income required.
eligibility: No documentation required; once per week per household

name: Rivertown Community Food Bank
description: Large-scale food bank distributing groceries to individuals and partner pantries citywide. Drive-through and walk-up available.
eligibility: Open to all Rivertown residents; ID recommended not required

name: Kids First Backpack Meal Program
description: Provides weekend backpacks of food for children who rely on school meals during the week.
eligibility: School-age children enrolled in Rivertown ISD

name: Rivertown Legal Aid Society
description: Free legal representation and advice for low-income residents on housing eviction defense and family law matters.
eligibility: Household income under 125% federal poverty line

name: Immigrant Rights Legal Clinic
description: Free consultations on immigration status asylum applications and family reunification cases. Spanish and Vietnamese speaking staff.
eligibility: No income requirement; appointment preferred

name: Tenant Rights Hotline
description: Free phone consultations for renters facing eviction unsafe housing conditions or landlord disputes.
eligibility: Free; open to all renters

name: Rivertown Financial Empowerment Center
description: Free one-on-one financial counseling covering budgeting debt reduction and credit repair.
eligibility: Free; no income requirement; appointment recommended

name: Emergency Utility Assistance Program
description: One-time financial assistance to prevent utility shutoff for qualifying low-income households.
eligibility: Income under 150% federal poverty line; utility shutoff notice required

name: Rivertown Rent Relief Fund
description: Emergency rental assistance grants for households at risk of eviction due to job loss or medical hardship.
eligibility: Proof of income loss or hardship required; income under 200% FPL

name: Little Sprouts Childcare Subsidy Program
description: Helps low-income families access subsidized childcare slots at partner daycare centers citywide.
eligibility: Income under 185% federal poverty line; must be employed or in school

name: Rivertown Parent Resource Center
description: Free parenting classes childcare referrals and diaper distribution for new and expecting parents.
eligibility: Free; open to all parents and caregivers

name: Rivertown Free Vision Clinic
description: Free eye exams and low-cost prescription glasses for uninsured residents.
eligibility: No insurance required; income-based discount on glasses

name: Rivertown Veterans Resource Hub
description: One-stop center connecting veterans to benefits housing assistance job placement and counseling.
eligibility: Must be a veteran or active military family member

name: Rivertown Senior Services Center
description: Meal delivery transportation assistance and social programs for adults 60 and older.
eligibility: Ages 60+; free or low-cost depending on program

name: Community Dental Outreach Van
description: Mobile dental unit offering free checkups and basic care at rotating locations, with focus on uninsured children.
eligibility: No insurance required; children prioritized; check schedule for location

name: Rivertown Free Legal Clinic - Family Law
description: Monthly walk-in clinic offering free advice on custody divorce and child support matters.
eligibility: Free; walk-in; no income requirement

name: Rivertown Sliding Scale Therapy Collective
description: Group of independent therapists offering sliding-scale individual therapy for adults, specializing in trauma and grief.
eligibility: Sliding scale $10-$60 per session; no insurance required

name: Rivertown Domestic Violence Legal Advocacy
description: Free legal help obtaining protective orders and navigating family court for survivors of abuse.
eligibility: Survivors of domestic violence; free and confidential

name: Rivertown 24-Hour Nurse Line
description: Free phone line staffed by nurses for medical questions and guidance on whether to seek emergency care.
eligibility: Free; open to all; no eligibility requirements

name: Rivertown Diaper Bank
description: Free diapers and baby wipes distributed monthly to families in need.
eligibility: Household with children under 3; income-based

name: Rivertown Job & Career Center
description: Free job placement resume help and career counseling services, including for those with unstable housing.
eligibility: Free; open to all Rivertown residents
